channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Mattress flytipped on Pavement, 10th April
item.description:
Mattress has been flytipped on the pavement at the junction of Orchard Road and Sundridge Avenue, next to Breaside school

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41-43, Orchard Road, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on rear of green utility box needs removing please, 10th April
item.description:
.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): North Park, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wood covering pavement, 10th April
item.description:
Stopping people using pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Morston Gardens, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on black bin needs removing please, 10th April
item.description:
.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Court Road, Eltham

item.title: Fly tipping issue, 10th April
item.description:
Large number of items left in front of the end garage, including mattress and lots of dismantled furniture.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mill Lane, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bikes, 10th April
item.description:
2 lime bikes dumped outside school gates

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Commerell Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bike was on the floor blocking taxi spot, 10th April
item.description:
Outside 106 great Russell street a bike was left blocking parking spaces and a tripping hazard

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 106, Great Russell Street, Bloomsbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked drain, 10th April
item.description:
Blocked drain (another further up the street). Water has nowhere to run but down the street and pools and floods the road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 112, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Park closed, 10th April
item.description:
This park, play area, has been closed for months. It is an important recreational space for this area and is used a lot. However, the children have not been able to access it as the area is taped off and the gates padlocked. When will this play area be reopened?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Riverside Road, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping again in St Peter's Lane, 10th April
item.description:
A pile of domestic refuse has been dumped on the side of the road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, St Peters Lane, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish been on same place Since 01-04-2026, 10th April
item.description:
Hackney Cpouncil has only Cleared the waste on 01-04-2026 very earlier in the morning before 6am and since then no generaL RUBBISH OR STREET CLEANING HAS TAKEN PLACE we had a digusting holiday with seeing all this dumped Rubbish

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rookwood Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped clothing & household items, 10th April
item.description:
Dumped clothing & household items

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Balgowan Street, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping on carriageway, 10th April
item.description:
Including glass on the carriageway on approach to bridge near this point
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bazalgette Way, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 17 Chatsworth Road nw2, 10th April
item.description:
Dangerous tree within 30 feet of railway line and it’s 100 feet tall !!! Needs a tree surveyor to give written report on risk ,!!!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chatsworth Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tax and MOT expired, 10th April
item.description:
Front end partially dismantled. Tax due 11/02/2026 MOT Expired on 15/12/2025 This van is in a Woolf Close parking bay. Located on the left as you turn right and pass the trees. see also 92704 and 9270544 all three are in adjacent parking bays.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolf Close, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent As I mentioned when I reported this originally, kids have now thrown the full oil containers into the bushes. You said you had cleared them but you haven't. They are now leaking oil! Please clear these up before even more damage is done, 10th April
item.description:
As I mentioned when I reported this originally, kids have now thrown the full oil containers into the bushes. You said you had cleared them but you haven't. They are now leaking oil! Please clear these up before even more damage is done! Thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Market Meadow, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Corner opposite 32 Claremont Road, 10th April
item.description:
Buses pass over this corner and needs board SMS kerb replacing

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Claremont Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead Fox, 10th April
item.description:
I am reporting again the dead fox that has still not been collected it is 4 weeks tomorrow its been dead & nothing has been done! I reported it myself on Tuesday & your report said it had been collected Wednesday which today is Friday & it is still there i brought todays News Paper as evidence that it is still there! The London Marathon is next weekend we are on the main route for the runners when the final runners get to our location they use the pavement where the dead fox is so they will either trip over it injuring themselves or others or step on it & after being stepped on a few times regardless of how long its been dead for it will burst open then runners will be running spreading its blood across the street not only traumatising themselves & others but then creating a bio hazard making millions of people sick but then costing more money because of having to hire a bio hazard team to ensure the area is cleaned correctly to prevent further spreading of unknown diseases

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shooters Hill Road, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: In the garden of 17 Chatsworth Road, 10th April
item.description:
Dangerous 100 foot eucalyptus tree very close to the railway line and reported to network rail It is leaning at a very alarming angle Network rail informed.03457114141 Ref260406-000053 they want too inspect with council in attendance

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chatsworth Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large series of potholes before roundabout, 10th April
item.description:
These potholes are very large and deep in the left hand lane before the roundabout going towards Croydon they have been there for many months and getting worse
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
